story.iWant: to modify my theme via `/theme-edit` with the same gestalt feedback loop used during setup
story.soThat: I can refine my brand settings without running full re-setup and see validated samples before committing changes

tasks.task: Implement Theme Editor Module - feedback interpretation logic and primitive updates
Implement Version Manager Module - theme-history backup before edits
Implement Sample Regenerator - 6 sample slides with updated theme
Implement Template Updater - regenerate templates if shape/layout primitives changed
Implement Theme Edit Workflow - 6-phase workflow.yaml and instructions.md
Implement Status Logger for Theme Edit - status.yaml history updates
Testing - Theme Edit Verification - all acceptance criteria

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Given a theme exists, when the user runs `/theme-edit`, then they can describe desired changes in natural language
The system interprets gestalt feedback (e.g., "warmer colors", "bolder fonts", "more minimal")
The system applies interpreted changes to theme primitives
Sample slides are regenerated with the updated theme for visual validation
The 6 sample slides demonstrate all theme primitives (title, list, flow, columns, callout, code)
User can approve changes or provide additional feedback
When user approves, theme.json is updated with changes
Templates are regenerated if shape/layout primitives changed
A version entry is created in theme-history before changes are applied
User can cancel editing to discard all changes

doc.title: Epic 6 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Defines Theme Editor module (interprets gestalt feedback), Sample Regenerator (6 slides), Version Manager (save before edit), and 6-phase workflow. Feedback interpretation logic maps "warmer colors" → shift hues, "bolder" → increase weights, etc.

doc.title: Project Architecture
doc.snippet: Theme at .slide-builder/theme.json, versions in theme-history/, samples in samples/, templates in templates/. status.yaml tracks last_action and history array. All timestamps ISO 8601.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: FR9: "modify existing theme via /theme-edit with same feedback loop". UX philosophy: "AI does work, user is creative director" with gestalt feedback, not micro-configuration. Target 1-3 feedback rounds.

doc.title: Epics and Stories
doc.snippet: User story and 10 acceptance criteria. Depends on Story 6.1 (Theme Loader, Theme Formatter patterns).

doc.title: Story 6.1: Theme Summary View
doc.snippet: Previous story established Theme Loader module, Theme Formatter with ANSI colors, 3-phase workflow pattern, status.yaml logging. Theme.json uses meta.extractedFrom (not sources). Layouts in theme.slides.layouts.

doc.title: Slide Builder Conventions
doc.snippet: Workflows in .slide-builder/workflows/{name}/ with workflow.yaml and instructions.md. Slash commands in .claude/commands/sb/. Theme at .slide-builder/theme.json.

file.reason: Active theme file to read, modify, and save. Contains colors, typography, shapes, components, slides.layouts, personality, and meta (with extractedFrom).
Existing workflow stub. References theme_file, theme_history paths. template: false (action-workflow). Needs expansion to 6-phase workflow per tech spec.
Existing basic instructions with 5 steps. Needs rewrite to 6-phase workflow: Load/Backup, Collect Feedback, Apply Changes, Regenerate Samples, Validation Loop, Save/Update Templates.
Story 6.1 reference for Theme Loader and Theme Formatter patterns. Reuse Phase 1 (Load Theme) and ANSI swatch generation for displaying current theme before edit.
Reference for sample deck generation (Phase 4), feedback loop (Phase 5), and frontend-design skill usage. Reuse sample slide specs and regeneration logic.
Track theme edit actions in history array. Update last_action and last_modified. Log "Theme edit started", "Theme v{N} saved", "Theme edited: {feedback}".
Existing command stub. Needs expansion with prerequisites, example usage, feedback examples, approval commands.
Story 6.1 reference for command documentation pattern. Footer suggests "/sb:theme-edit" for modifications.
Version storage directory. Save current theme.json before edits as theme-v{N}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.json. Create if doesn't exist.
Sample slides directory. Regenerate 6 samples (01-title.html through 06-technical.html) with updated theme for validation.
Layout templates directory. Regenerate if shape/layout primitives changed during edit.

ecosystem.name: claude-code
ecosystem.package: Generate/regenerate sample slides with professional design
Save theme.json, versions, samples, templates, status.yaml

constraints.constraint: Workflow.yaml + instructions.md structure following BMAD pattern
status.yaml tracks mode, last_action, history with ISO 8601 timestamps
Theme at .slide-builder/theme.json, history in theme-history/, samples in samples/, templates in templates/
Version saved BEFORE changes applied - always have rollback option
Cancel during edit discards changes, restores from version
Templates regenerated ONLY if shape/layout primitives change
Modify existing theme via /theme-edit with same gestalt feedback loop
Theme feedback loop iteration should feel responsive (<30s sample regeneration)
All operations local - no brand assets transmitted externally
After 3 feedback rounds, offer direct theme.json editing as escape hatch
Layouts are in theme.slides.layouts (title, content, split, data)

interface.name: Theme Editor Module
interface.signature: Input: currentTheme (ThemeJson), feedback (string) → Output: updatedTheme (ThemeJson), changes (string[])

interface.name: Version Manager Module
interface.signature: Save: theme → versionFile, newVersionNumber. Restore: versionNumber → restoredTheme, error

interface.name: Sample Regenerator Module
interface.signature: Input: ThemeJson → Output: 6 sample HTML files in .slide-builder/samples/

interface.name: Template Updater Module
interface.signature: Input: oldTheme, newTheme → Output: updated template files if shapes changed

interface.name: status.yaml History
interface.signature: Append: { action: string, timestamp: ISO8601 }. Update: last_action, last_modified

interface.name: frontend-design Skill
interface.signature: Skill(skill="frontend-design") - for sample slide generation with professional design

tests.standards: Per Epic 6 Tech Spec Test Strategy: Manual verification per module for unit tests, end-to-end testing for full workflows, manual AC verification for acceptance. Regression tests ensure Epic 2 /setup still works. Key test approach: run /theme-edit, provide gestalt feedback, verify changes applied, samples regenerated, approval flow works, cancel restores original.

locations.location: .slide-builder/samples/ - verify 6 sample slides regenerated with updated theme

ideas.idea: Run /theme-edit with valid theme, verify prompt for feedback appears
Provide "warmer colors" feedback, verify hue changes described in output
After feedback, verify theme primitives changed (colors shifted)
After feedback, verify 6 samples regenerated in .slide-builder/samples/
Check samples cover: title, list, flow, columns, callout, code
After samples, verify approval prompt with options: feedback/approved/cancel
Type "approved", verify theme.json updated with changes
Change shape primitives (corner radius), verify templates regenerated
Before edit starts, verify current theme saved to theme-history/theme-v{N}-{date}.json
Type "cancel", verify theme unchanged from pre-edit state
No theme exists: verify error "No theme found. Run /setup first"
Feedback exceeds 3 rounds: verify escape hatch offered for direct JSON editing
ANSI not supported: verify fallback to plain text display
